content.claim: Hungary's 2004 Act on Multi-Functional Micro-Regional Local Government Associations accelerated school closures/amalgamations by shifting state incentives and funding mechanisms, creating a domestic precedent for institutional-change effects distinct from demographic pressure alone.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Causal attribution of closure acceleration to 2004 Act not quantified; pre/post comparison not provided
Does not isolate effect of incentive change from simultaneous macroeconomic or demographic shifts
Transferability to Polish context limited by different municipal structures and funding mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
